food_subgroup: Crustacean dish — whole crab cooked in chilli tomato sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'crab dish' class (FOODON_03311737); no specific Singapore chilli crab term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers cooked crabs as a category. No specific chilli crab preparation code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Singapore chilli crab or equivalent prepared crab dish.

serving.description: 1 whole crab (~500 g edible portion with sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of Singapore chilli crab is one whole mud crab weighing approximately 500 g including sauce, typically shared between one to two diners and served with mantou (fried buns) for dipping.

nutrient_source.title: Chilli Crab — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central Crab (cooked) adapted with sauce components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists chilli crab as a Singaporean dish. Per-100g values are derived from HPB Foodo nutrient data for chilli crab (cooked whole crab with chilli tomato sauce), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked blue crab (NDB 15144) and USDA sauce component data to produce a composite estimate. The serving size of 500 g reflects one whole crab with sauce as commonly served in Singapore.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed seafood dishes with sauce — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for tomato-based sauces and cooked shellfish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore chilli crab in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 45 (low) based on the following reasoning: the dish is predominantly protein and fat from crab meat (GI effectively 0 for protein/fat components), with a moderate carbohydrate contribution from the chilli-tomato sauce containing sugar and starch thickener. Tomato-based sauces with sugar have a GI in the range of 35–55 per University of Sydney database analogues. A composite GI of 45 is defensible. GL per 500 g serving = (45 × 26.0 g carbohydrate per 500 g) / 100 = 11.7.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2400 mg per 500 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid chilli crab due to its very high sodium content (~2400 mg per standard 500 g serving), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion reduction and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from chilli-tomato sauce with added sugar; low GI (estimated 45)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chilli crab has a low estimated glycemic index (~45) and moderate glycemic load (~11.7 per 500 g serving), making it a manageable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without the accompanying fried mantou buns; monitoring total carbohydrate intake from the sauce is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate cholesterol content; beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from crab
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While crab meat provides beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and lean protein, the high sodium content (~2400 mg per serving) and moderate dietary cholesterol (~390 mg per 500 g serving) are concerns for individuals with cardiovascular disease; limiting portion size and frequency of consumption is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Singapore Chilli Crab (辣椒螃蟹, Ketam Cili) is one of Singapore's most iconic national dishes and a cornerstone of Singaporean culinary identity. The dish features a whole mud crab (Scylla serrata), typically weighing 500–800 g, that is wok-fried and then simmered in a rich, semi-thick sauce made from fresh chillies, tomato paste, fermented soybean paste, garlic, ginger, shallots, egg, sugar, and soy sauce. Despite its name, the dish is not overwhelmingly spicy — the sauce is savoury, mildly sweet, tangy, and moderately spicy, with a silky egg-drop texture. The crab meat is tender, juicy, and slightly sweet, absorbing the complex sauce flavours. Chilli crab is traditionally served with deep-fried or steamed mantou (Chinese buns) for dipping into the sauce. It is a popular dish at seafood restaurants along Singapore's East Coast Seafood Centre and Boat Quay. Nutritionally, chilli crab is a high-protein, moderate-fat dish with significant sodium content from the sauce. It provides vitamin B12, zinc, selenium, and phosphorus from the crab meat. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low), though the sauce contributes moderate carbohydrates and added sugar. Clinically, the high sodium content (~2400 mg per 500 g serving) is the primary concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not halal-certified in most traditional preparations due to the use of fermented soybean paste and potential cross-contamination. It is a crustacean allergen food and contains egg and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chilli crab?
What is the GI of chilli crab?
How much sodium in chilli crab?
What crab is used in Singapore chilli crab?
Is chilli crab high in cholesterol?
Chilli crab vs black pepper crab

confidence_reason: HPB Foodo lists chilli crab as a Singaporean dish with nutrient data. Per-100g values are adapted from HPB Foodo and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy cooked crab data plus sauce component estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database analogues as no direct GI measurement for Singapore chilli crab exists in the published literature. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite/adapted nature of the nutrient derivation and the analogous GI estimate.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this dish. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this dish. 3. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in cooked crab and vegetable oil-based sauce per USDA SR Legacy data. 4. The food_name has been normalised to the canonical English form 'Chilli Crab' from the input's menu-style entry 'Chilli Crab x2 (~500grams x 2)'; the x2 portion descriptor is captured in aliases. 5. The cuisine tag has been corrected from 'Malaysian' to 'Singaporean' as chilli crab is a nationally recognised Singaporean dish originating in Singapore, not Malaysia, per HPB and standard culinary references. 6. GI is estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous University of Sydney GI Database entries for tomato-sauce-based seafood dishes; no direct peer-reviewed GI measurement for Singapore chilli crab was found. 7. Halal status is false as traditional chilli crab preparations typically use fermented soybean paste (tau cheo) and may not be prepared in halal-certified kitchens; the source restaurant (George Town Tze Char) is a zi char establishment not listed as halal-certified. 8. Serving size of 500 g is based on the input description of one crab (~500 g) and standard restaurant portion norms in Singapore.
